original_abstract: Commemorating the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project, we present a high-performance AI/TP system capable of automatically proving approximately 60% of Mizar theorems in a standard hammer setting. When the automated provers are restricted to the specific premises utilized in human-authored proofs, this success rate increases to 75%. We detail the methodologies and large-scale experimental frameworks that underpin these results, highlighting the roles of the E and Vampire theorem provers, along with their specialized neural extensions, ENIGMA and Deepire. A key component of our approach is an incremental training loop that alternates between generating a corpus of millions of ATP proofs and refining the AI models based on this data. Finally, we showcase a selection of challenging Mizar problems that are now solvable through full automation.
humanized_abstract: We present a high-performance AI/TP system that on the occasion of the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project can automatically prove about 60% of the Mizar theorems in a hammer mode. This rate rises to 75% if the ATPs are limited to using only the statements in the premises of the human-made proofs. We explain our techniques and provide information on the resources needed for our large-scale experiments. These include the E and Vampire theorem provers, each enhanced with two neural components, namely ENIGMA and Deepire. The proofs in the training corpora that amount to millions are produced within an incremental learning loop. We present several representative examples of relatively difficult Mizar theorems that can now be proved fully automatically.

document.output: We present a high-performance AI/TP system that on the occasion of the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project can automatically prove about 60% of the Mizar theorems in a hammer mode. We explain our techniques and provide information on the resources needed for our large-scale experiments. These include the E and Vampire theorem provers, each enhanced with two neural components, namely ENIGMA and Deepire. The proofs in the training corpora that amount to millions are produced within an incremental learning loop. We present several representative examples of relatively difficult Mizar theorems that can now be proved fully automatically.
